tweet: These unilateral actions — not taken by the state legislatures but judges and election officials — are not supported by the Constitution. Therefore, I will object. 6/6

name: Congressman Michael Cloud
tweet: In many states, however, judges and election officials at varying levels instituted their own election processes. These new processes implemented, sometimes weeks leading up to Election Day, violated established state election laws. 5/6

name: Congressman Michael Cloud
tweet: Article II of the Constitution (reaffirmed in the 12th Amendment) makes clear that it is the state legislatures who define how electors are selected — in other words, how elections are managed within their state. 4/6

name: Congressman Michael Cloud
tweet: The Constitution establishes the framework upon which our Republic rests. The constitutional restraints on those in authority are not suspended in crisis — but are established, in part, to ensure that even in a crisis those authorities remain defined. 3/6

name: Congressman Michael Cloud
tweet: My questions to AG Barr: What are you doing to ensure the integrity of the voting & counting process right now? Will you commit to using all resources at your disposal to ensure only legal votes are being counted & being counted in a fully transparent manner immediately?

name: Congressman Michael Cloud
tweet: The U.S. Marshals Service is also available to ensure that judicial orders are carried out. When it comes to a federal election, it should not matter whether those judicial orders come from a state or federal court.

name: Congressman Michael Cloud
tweet: The letter reads "@TheJusticeDept's Civil Rights Division Voting Section’s responsibility is to ensure that the right to vote is sacred. This not only means access to the ballot box, but it also means ensuring that no one’s vote is devalued by any means of voter fraud."

name: Congressman Michael Cloud
tweet: The allegations of irregularities in the vote counting process should be concerning to everyone. I asked AG Barr to use the resources of @TheJusticeDept  to ensure that the process is conducted in a manner that is fully consistent with state and federal law.  @realDonaldTrump  https://t.co/uRghDtEVBJ

name: Congressman Michael Cloud
tweet: It's been three years since Hurricane Harvey wreaked havoc on Texas, and we are still working with some communities in TX-27 to fully recover to this day. My bills would encourage a culture of savings by making it easier to be financially prepared. (6/6)

name: Congressman Michael Cloud
tweet: The coronavirus pandemic has caused disastrous economic impacts. Despite borrowing and spending trillions of federal dollars to provide direct financial assistance to the American people, many families still find themselves in precarious financial positions. (5/6)

name: Congressman Michael Cloud
tweet: With these new emergency savings accounts, individuals would be able to deposit and withdraw money from that account tax-free when they use it for qualified expenses, such as mortgages, rent, utilities, property taxes, child care, and health care. (4/6)

name: Congressman Michael Cloud
tweet: The second bill would provide small businesses with the ability to save up to 25% of their payroll expenses tax-free in an emergency savings account. (3/6)

name: Congressman Michael Cloud
tweet: The first bill would provide individual taxpayers with the ability to save up to $5,000 per year by making tax-free contributions to an emergency savings account. A married couple would be able to save up to $10,000. (2/6)

tweet: I recently introduced two bills: the Emergency Savings Account Act and the Small Business Emergency Savings Account Act to help Americans to better prepare for the future. (1/6)   https://t.co/48TzPpLB9Y

tweet: In today's “compromise” – Congress could not find $5.7 billion for a barrier that Customs and Border Protection has requested to help end the humanitarian crisis on our border.  Yet the bill contains more than $12 billion in international assistance.  #CongressIsBroken

name: Congressman Michael Cloud
tweet: Party leaders sent us an 1169-page bill this morning. Vote is this evening.  As of 1pm, congressional offices were calling each other to see if anyone even knew the total cost  I cosponsored a 1-week extension to fund the gov't while we #ReadTheBill & negotiate a real compromise
